audit_items.proof_trace: All dynamic allocations (calloc, strdup) have explicit null checks with early returns before any dereference. argc bounds are validated before each argv[i] access. Context pointers from rewrite_context_find/create are null-checked (L157-163, L197-205). The assert at L250 guards an internal programming invariant ('Default context MUST exist in a properly initialized struct rewrite_info') that is not attacker-controlled data, and the dereference of that value occurs inside rewrite_rule_compile (not within config.c itself). No external LDAP input path leads to a null dereference within config.c's own code.
